words.definition: a self-centered person who believes in self-interest
words.example: An egoist always asks 'What's in it for me?' before acting.

words.definition: an arrogant talker who brags about accomplishments
words.example: The egotist spent the entire dinner talking about his promotions.

words.definition: thinking only of oneself, self-centered
words.example: He is so egocentric that he thinks every conversation is about him.

words.definition: someone morbidly obsessed with themselves
words.example: Her extreme vanity made her behave like an egomaniac.

words.definition: a person unselfishly concerned for the welfare of others
words.example: The altruist spent her weekends volunteering at the shelter.

words.definition: take turns or skip one and take the other
words.example: We will alternate driving shifts to avoid getting tired.

words.definition: a change or modification
words.example: She made some alterations to the dress so it fit perfectly.

words.definition: a heated argument or dispute
words.example: The minor accident led to an altercation between the drivers.

words.definition: another self; a secondary personality
words.example: Bruce Wayne's alterego is Batman.

words.definition: a shy, reticent person focused on their own thoughts
words.example: As an introvert, she preferred reading a book over going to a loud party.

words.definition: an outgoing, socially confident person
words.example: The extrovert made friends with everyone in the room within minutes.

words.definition: a person whose personality has features of both
words.example: He is an ambivert who enjoys socializing but also needs quiet time.

words.definition: to turn upside down or inside out
words.example: Invert the jar to let the liquid drain completely.

words.definition: to return to a previous state or habit
words.example: After a week of clean eating, he reverted to his junk food habits.

words.definition: a person who dislikes humankind and avoids society
words.example: The hermit was accused of being a misanthrope who hated visitors.

words.definition: a person who dislikes or is prejudiced against women
words.example: His offensive remarks revealed him to be a misogynist.

words.definition: a person who hates marriage
words.example: The lifelong bachelor was a proud misogamist.

words.definition: showing hatred or distrust of humankind
words.example: He held a misanthropic view of world events.

words.definition: hatred of or prejudice against women
words.example: The committee worked to eliminate misogyny in the workplace.

words.definition: a doctor who specializes in skin diseases
words.example: The dermatologist prescribed a cream for his eczema.

words.definition: inflammation of the skin
words.example: Touching the poison ivy caused severe dermatitis.

words.definition: the outer layer of skin
words.example: The sun burned the epidermis of his shoulders.

words.definition: one who stuffs and mounts skins of dead animals
words.example: The hunter took the deer head to a taxidermist.

words.definition: a thick-skinned animal (like an elephant)
words.example: Elephants and rhinos are classified as pachyderms.

words.definition: a medical practitioner specializing in mental illness
words.example: The psychiatrist diagnosed the patient's chemical imbalance.

words.definition: a specialist in the study of the mind and behavior
words.example: The psychologist conducted research on cognitive development.

words.definition: suffering from a chronic mental disorder with abnormal behavior
words.example: The thriller featured a psychopathic villain.

words.definition: physical illness caused or aggravated by mental factors
words.example: His chronic stomach aches were largely psychosomatic.

words.definition: the human soul, mind, or spirit
words.example: Exploring the depths of the human psyche is fascinating.

words.definition: a doctor who specializes in eye anatomy and diseases
words.example: She visited the ophthalmologist for regular eye pressure tests.

words.definition: the branch of medicine concerned with study and treatment of eye disorders
words.example: He chose to specialize in ophthalmology after medical school.

words.definition: relating to the eye and its diseases
words.example: The pharmacist recommended ophthalmic drops for dryness.

words.definition: an instrument for inspecting the retina and parts of the eye
words.example: The doctor used an ophthalmoscope to peer into the pupil.

words.definition: a dentist who specializes in straightening teeth
words.example: The orthodontist tightened her braces.

words.definition: a specialist in correcting deformities of bones or muscles
words.example: The orthopedist cast his broken leg.

words.definition: conforming to what is traditionally accepted as right
words.example: His orthodox methods produced reliable, if boring, results.

words.definition: the conventional spelling system of a language
words.example: English orthography is notoriously difficult due to silent letters.
